journal_entry.reason: Record purchase of office equipment (desks and computers) for cash and motor vehicle (crane-equipped truck) on account. Office desks and computers are combined as they are the same asset class with the same treatment. The truck is recorded separately as motor vehicles. Cash is reduced by the amount paid ($5,500), and trade payables increased by the amount owed ($250,000).

output_debit_classifier.reason: Atlas Van Lines acquired three types of property, plant, and equipment. Office desks ($2,000) and computers ($3,500) are office equipment paid in cash, totaling $5,500. The crane-equipped truck ($250,000) is a motor vehicle for moving operations purchased on credit. Under IAS 16, all items are recognized at cost. The transaction involves two asset increases (office equipment and motor vehicles), one asset decrease (cash), and one liability increase (trade payables).
